title: “Could I be an Asian girl?”: Racist Fantasy in HBO’s “The White Lotus”
description:
Portraying the fall of the mighty is easy money, low-hanging fruit. Eat the rich! What’s riskier and harder to swallow are the moments when the viewer comes into contact with the white elite’s racist fantasies, which include the tendencies of prestige TV and its insufficient depictions of people of color.

The post “Could I be an Asian girl?”: Racist Fantasy in HBO’s “The White Lotus”<
hook: White Lotus doesn't just critique the rich—it traps you inside their racist fantasies, and that's where it gets genuinely uncomfortable.

title: They Would Not Dream of Flowers: Translating Through the Tehran Blackout
description: "There, in that forced isolation, I sat translating a story about death. Meanwhile, the real thing operated just outside my window."
hook: A translator working through Tehran's blackout, rendering death on the page while real violence darkens her window—language as witness.

title: Baldwin by Nicholas Boggs review – the relationships that drove a genius
description:
A new biography puts Baldwin’s sexuality – and the men he loved – front and centre

Today, James Baldwin’s legacy seems assured, but this wasn’t always the case. His critical reputation, already on the wane in his lifetime, declined after his death in 1987. On the publication of the Library of America’s Collected Essays and Early Novels & Stories a decade later, Michael Anderson, writing in the
hook: Baldwin's biography finally centers the men he loved, not as footnotes to genius but as the actual architecture of his life and work.

title: Negative Tennis
description: Any intrigue is not over a win or loss, but tiny details: Can the opponent break Sinner’s serve? Reach a break point? Win more than two or three points against Sinner’s first serve? Get that first serve back at all, and survive the devastating follow-up forehand if they do?
hook: Tennis criticism that ignores winners and losers entirely—obsessed instead with the microscopic drama of a single serve.

title: Beyond a ‘Reasonable Doubt’
description: “Revisiting Jay-Z's hustler masterpiece—released on his own label at age 25—in the rapper's billionaire era.”
hook: Revisiting Reasonable Doubt as Jay-Z's indie masterpiece in an era where he's a billionaire—ambition as autobiography.
